name: Andrew Cyrille
abstract: Andrew Charles Cyrille (born November 10, 1939) is an American avant-garde jazz drummer. Throughout his career, he has performed both as a leader and a sideman in the bands of Walt Dickerson and Cecil Taylor, among others.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Leroy Jenkins Ensemble
America Salutes Moscow: 850th Anniversary of the City of Moscow/Benefit: American University in Moscow
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Dave Douglas Jazz Workshop in Performance

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I went just last week over to the North Sea Festival in the Hague to play with Mal again, this time with his trio, with Andrew Cyrille, Reggie Workman, Mal, and I was special guest.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to people like Cecil and Henry Grimes and all those guys who were stretching out that stuff, right, and Sunny Murray, another drummer who was doing that, and Milford Graves, Andrew Cyrille, it was all kinds of just trying to play something different
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...What about in the early ’60s when Sonny Murray and Andrew Cyrille and Milford Graves... What . . .? [INTERVIEWEE] What about it?
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Andrew Cyrille knows how to do that too, does that well. I heard him with Cecil Taylor
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
